# Spec Review Checklist: GroundedGenerator

**Purpose**: Self-review leve do autor (spec.md + plan.md), cobertura balanceada, antes de rodar `/speckit-tasks`
**Created**: 2026-07-15
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. Items test the quality of the requirements themselves (completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability), not the implementation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are requirements defined for the case where the model's structured output itself is malformed or fails schema validation (distinct from a network/availability failure)? [Gap] ‚Äî Resolved: new FR-012 (spec.md) requires a distinct operational error when the model's response fails schema validation or is refused; `InvalidModelOutputError` added in data-model.md, contracts/GroundedGenerator.md, and tasks.md (T008, T012, T014).
- [x] CHK002 - Is behavior specified for an empty or missing `question`, analogous to the existing empty-`context` rule? [Gap, Spec ¬ßEdge Cases] ‚Äî Resolved: new Edge Case bullet (spec.md) states an empty/missing `question` is rejected immediately as invalid usage, without calling the model; reflected in data-model.md, contracts/GroundedGenerator.md, and tasks.md (T017, T022).
- [x] CHK003 - Are requirements defined for concurrent/parallel invocations of the same component instance (e.g., statelessness guarantees)? [Gap] ‚Äî Resolved: new Assumptions bullet (spec.md) states the component is stateless with no mutable state shared between calls, making concurrent calls to the same instance safe; reflected in contracts/GroundedGenerator.md rule 7.
- [x] CHK004 - Is the dependency on a token-estimation mechanism (implied by FR-011) documented as an assumption or constraint in the spec? [Dependency, Gap, Spec ¬ßFR-011] ‚Äî Resolved: documented in research.md ("Detec√ß√£o de contexto que excede o limite do modelo (FR-011)"). Per Speckit's spec/plan separation, a token-estimation mechanism is an implementation detail and correctly belongs in research.md/plan.md, not spec.md ‚Äî adding it to spec.md would violate the "no implementation details" guideline.

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 - Is "informa√ß√£o parcialmente relacionada" (partial context) given an objective decision rule, or is the sufficiency judgment left entirely undefined beyond "avaliar se √© suficiente"? [Ambiguity, Spec ¬ßEdge Cases] ‚Äî Resolved: the Edge Case bullet (spec.md) now explicitly documents this as an intentionally accepted model-judgment call (no deterministic rule defined), routed through the same FR-002 sufficiency mechanism as every other case.
- [x] CHK006 - Is "trecho literal" (literal excerpt) precise enough to distinguish an exact substring from a lightly reworded paraphrase of the context? [Clarity, Spec ¬ßFR-001] ‚Äî Resolved: data-model.md's `ExtractedFact` entity states explicitly "Deve ser um trecho literal existente no `context` fornecido ‚Äî n√£o par√°frase," which is in scope per this checklist's own stated coverage of spec.md + plan.md.
- [x] CHK007 - Is the starting point and completion condition for SC-003's "menos de 30 minutos" claim defined (e.g., from first reading docs to first successful call)? [Measurability, Spec ¬ßSC-003] ‚Äî Resolved: SC-003 (spec.md) now specifies the start (reading the quickstart integration documentation) and end (first successful functional response) points.

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK008 - Do FR-008 and the Assumptions section's "por exemplo via LangChain" wording still match the technical direction recorded in plan.md (standalone `openai` client, no LangChain dependency)? [Conflict, Spec ¬ßFR-008, Spec ¬ßAssumptions] ‚Äî Resolved: FR-008 (spec.md:86) was rewritten to describe the optional injected `openai` client / internal creation from `apiKey`/`model`, matching plan.md exactly. No LangChain reference remains in FR-008.
- [x] CHK009 - Is the assumption that "o modelo de chat usado pelo componente j√° est√° configurado e autenticado pelo desenvolvedor" consistent with plan.md's constructor-level `apiKey`/`model` configuration, where the component itself manages the client? [Conflict, Spec ¬ßAssumptions] ‚Äî Resolved: the Assumptions bullet (spec.md:113) was rewritten to state the component manages its own OpenAI authentication (injected `client` or internally built from `apiKey`/`OPENAI_API_KEY`/`model`), consistent with plan.md and data-model.md's `GroundedCallConfig`.

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK010 - Can SC-001's "pelo menos 95% dos casos" be objectively measured without the spec defining the test set's composition or minimum size? [Measurability, Spec ¬ßSC-001] ‚Äî Resolved: tasks.md T034 now defines a concrete evaluation set (‚â•20 cases: empty, off-topic, partially related, contradictory) and asserts the measured fallback rate is ‚â•95%, giving SC-001 an objective, executable measurement.
- [x] CHK011 - Is a verification method implied or referenced for SC-002's "100% rastre√°veis... sem acr√©scimo de informa√ß√£o externa" claim? [Measurability, Spec ¬ßSC-002] ‚Äî Resolved: tasks.md T035 defines a concrete evaluation set (‚â•20 sufficient-context cases) and an explicit traceability check (terms/phrases in `finalAnswer` must appear in `extractedFacts`), asserted across 100% of the set.
- [x] CHK012 - Is SC-004's "sem inspe√ß√£o de c√≥digo ou logs adicionais" claim testable independently of implementation choices? [Measurability, Spec ¬ßSC-004] ‚Äî Resolved: `GroundedCallResult` (data-model.md/tasks.md T006) makes `usedFallback` a required field of the public result type, and both branches (T018 sufficient-path, T025 fallback-path) are asserted by tests (T015, T020) to always set it ‚Äî the claim is structurally guaranteed and directly testable without log inspection.

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK013 - Are requirements defined for invalid `GeneratorConfig` values beyond a missing fallback (e.g., invalid/empty model identifier)? [Gap, Spec ¬ßFR-005] ‚Äî Resolved: FR-005 (spec.md) now requires immediate construction failure for any other required/malformed config field (e.g., an empty `model` identifier), not just a missing fallback; reflected in tasks.md T009/T013.
- [x] CHK014 - Are retry/recovery expectations for `OperationalError` scenarios addressed, or explicitly left to the calling developer? [Gap, Spec ¬ßFR-010] ‚Äî Resolved: new Assumptions bullet (spec.md) states the component does not retry automatically on any `OperationalError`; retry policy is the consuming developer's responsibility. Reflected in data-model.md, contracts/GroundedGenerator.md, and tasks.md (T012, T014).

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK015 - Is a safety margin (vs. the model's exact token limit) specified for the FR-011 context-overflow check, or is behavior undefined right at the boundary? [Gap, Spec ¬ßFR-011] ‚Äî Resolved: FR-011 (spec.md) now requires a safety margin below the model's exact limit (exact value left to plan.md/research.md); reflected in tasks.md T007/T010/T014.
- [x] CHK016 - Is behavior defined for a `context` that consists only of irrelevant/off-topic text (distinct from empty/blank, already covered)? [Coverage, Spec ¬ßEdge Cases] ‚Äî Resolved: FR-004's "nenhum trecho relevante puder ser extra√≠do" already subsumes fully off-topic context (zero relevant facts extracted ‚Üí fallback), and tasks.md T034's evaluation set explicitly includes an "off-topic" test category.

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK017 - Are timeout/latency expectations defined that distinguish a "slow but eventually successful" call from an "unavailable" one for FR-010 purposes? [Gap, NFR] ‚Äî Resolved: new Assumptions bullet (spec.md) explicitly states performance/latency targets are out of scope for this feature, fixing the previously dangling cross-reference from plan.md's Performance Goals section.

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK018 - Is the assumption "suporte a provedores de linguagem al√©m do j√° utilizado... est√° fora do escopo" still accurate given plan.md's decision to depend specifically on the OpenAI client (not an arbitrary already-configured model)? [Assumption, Spec ¬ßAssumptions] ‚Äî Resolved: the Assumptions bullet (spec.md) was reworded to "Suporte a provedores de linguagem al√©m da API OpenAI est√° fora do escopo desta feature," matching the actual OpenAI-specific design instead of the old reuse-existing-model framing.

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK019 - Is there a defined resolution for how a model response that omits or misclassifies `sufficient_context` (neither clearly true nor false) should be handled ‚Äî as fallback, as `OperationalError`, or undefined? [Ambiguity, Gap] ‚Äî Resolved: omission is structurally impossible (`sufficient_context` is a required boolean field in the `zodResponseFormat`/`strict: true` schema, tasks.md T014/T016); the "misclassifies as true with no facts" case is explicitly caught by FR-004 and tested in T022 (forces fallback regardless of the model's own `sufficient_context` value); a false-negative misclassification only causes an unnecessary but safe fallback, consistent with the component's conservative design.

## Notes

- Escopo: Q1=C (cobertura balanceada de todo o spec), Q2=self-review leve, Q3=autor, antes de `/speckit-tasks`.
